title: AI Code Review Issue Schema
description: Defines the structure for an issue reported by the AI code review process.

issue_id.description: Identifier of the rule that was violated.

rule_id.description: Identifier of the rule that was violated.

lang.description: Language(s) this rule applies to, inherited from the rule definition.

severity.description: Severity of the issue, mapped from rule's 'sev' (e.g., B, C, H, M, L).

category.description: Category of the issue, mapped from rule's 'cat'.

file_path.description: Path to the file where the issue was found, relative to project root.

start_line.description: The 1-based line number where the issue starts.

end_line.description: The 1-based line number where the issue ends. Can be same as start_line.

start_column.description: The 0-based column number where the issue starts (optional).

end_column.description: The 0-based column number where the issue ends (optional).

message.description: A concise summary of the issue. Can be a templated message from the rule, filled with context.

detailed_explanation.description: A more detailed explanation of the issue, often generated by an AI based on the rule's 'rat' and code context. Explains 'why' and the impact.

code_snippet.description: The actual code snippet that violates the rule.

metric_values.description: Key-value pairs of metrics that triggered the rule, including thresholds if applicable.

suggested_action.description: The recommended action from the rule's 'act' (e.g., RQR, RCM, FIX).

enforcement.description: Gate behavior declared by the rule.

autofix_available.description: Indicates if an automated or AI-assisted fix is potentially available.

autofix_suggestion.description: Details for the autofix, if available. Structure depends on 'autofix.type' from the rule.

description.description: A human-readable description of the suggested fix or the command to run.

payload.description: Data needed for the autofix, e.g., a patch, a script path, or a prompt template.

rule_version.description: Version of the ruleset from which this rule originates (e.g., '2025-12-prime-x3-cn-optimized-v1').

scan_timestamp.description: Timestamp of when the scan was performed and this issue was generated.
